hushProfile.name: Social-media declutter
hushProfile.description: Removes algorithmic noise from the big social sites: promoted posts, 'Suggested for you' insertions, trending-topics sidebars, 'Who to follow' rails. Designed for users who visit social sites for specific reasons (watching a subscription feed, reading mentions) and don't want the engagement-optimized extras. Selectors favor stable attributes and custom-element tag names over utility classes (Reddit's shreddit-* tags, Twitter's data-testid). More fragile on Facebook/Instagram/LinkedIn where class names are build-hashed; expect to tune those quarterly.

remove.comment: Brand Affiliate / promoted posts. Uses :has() to target the post only when it contains the promoted-content flag.
Reddit's algorithmic 'communities you might like' insertions in the feed.
Reddit-authored custom element for the Games section cross-promo.
Distinguished-post inserts (admin / moderator pinned promos). Opinionated - some users want these; if you do, delete this entry.

hide.comment: Sidebar widgets that nag you to check out other subreddits / events / etc. Hide rather than Remove so the sidebar scaffolding stays intact.
Aria-label variant for the trending timeline aside.

remove.comment: Twitter's promoted-tweet marker. The parent article is the promoted tweet; removing the tracking placement typically collapses the container.
Belt and braces: the whole tweet article when it carries placementTracking.

hide.comment: Same DOM shape as twitter.com since they're the same app served under two hostnames.

remove.comment: Ad slots in the homepage and subscription feeds.
Topic-context 'statement banners' YouTube inserts around certain search results.

hide.comment: Algorithmic 'shelves' inserted into subscription / home feeds. Opinionated - disables 'recently uploaded' groupings too; delete this entry if you want those.
Shorts shelf in the feed. Opinionated - some users want Shorts in-feed; if you do, delete this entry.

remove.comment: Facebook's Sponsored-post marker. Facebook has historically obfuscated this label in response to cosmetic filtering, so expect breakage; re-audit quarterly. No :has() wrapper because Facebook's post tree is deeply nested and :has() perf is bad at that scale.

hide.comment: Suggested posts in the main feed. Class-based selectors on Instagram break every few weeks; aria-label is slightly more stable.
Explore-injected content in the main feed. Opinionated - if you rely on Explore embeds, delete this entry.
Promoted posts in the feed. The data-ad-banner attribute is LinkedIn's own marker for sponsored content.

remove.comment: TikTok uses data-e2e attributes (end-to-end test selectors) that survive CSS-class obfuscation; feed-ad is the in-feed ad marker.
